findings.description: mask_middle with default visible=4 leaks all-but-one character of any 9-char value (e.g. 'abcd*fghi'), so masking a short secret/token reveals nearly all of it while the docstring promises it is protected.

The 'fully masked' guard only triggers when len(value) <= visible*2, so values just above that threshold expose the vast majority of characters, contradicting the docstring's stated leak-prevention intent.

slugify returns an empty string for input that is entirely non-ASCII (Cyrillic/CJK/emoji), silently collapsing distinct inputs to '' and risking key/identifier collisions for callers using slugs as IDs.

truncate counts code points via len(), so its 'result never exceeds the limit' guarantee is character-based and can still exceed a visual/grapheme or byte budget when suffix or text contains combining/multi-byte characters.
pluralize treats abs(count)==1 as singular, so count=-1 yields the grammatically dubious '-1 item' while 0 correctly yields plural — an undocumented edge in the count-to-noun contract.
No function validates input type; passing None to collapse_whitespace/slugify/truncate/indent_block raises an unguarded AttributeError/TypeError rather than a documented contract error.
levenshtein allocates a fresh O(len(b)) list for every row of a; correct but a single preallocated rolling buffer would avoid per-row allocation churn on large inputs.
